title: rubus centrobohemicus
intro: Rubus centrobohemicus, also known as Bohemian black raspberry, is a perennial plant species in the rose family, characterized by its dark purple berries and thorny stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus centrobohemicus include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in jams, preserves, and other food products.
